title: SQL WHERE -1 in (-1) issue?
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE department ( department_id INT NOT NULL, department_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL );
CREATE TABLE employee ( employee_id INT NOT NULL, manager_id INT NULL, department_id INT NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY (department_id) REFERENCES department(department_id) );
INSERT INTO employee (employee_id, manager_id, department_id) VALUES (50, 70, 1);
SELECT employee_id, manager_id FROM employee WHERE employee_id = 60 AND (manager_id IN (-1) OR -1 IN (-1));
key_bug_features: Using -1 in IN clause with NULL values
